scope.summary: Implement a dedicated UV editor viewport panel in the Tailor front-end showing all island placements in atlas space with per-island selection, zoom/pan, and island highlight synced to the 2D pattern editor selection
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated UV editor panel renders all UvIslandPlacement rows for the active garment in normalized atlas [0,1]^2 space
Selecting a panel in the 2D pattern editor highlights the corresponding UV island in the UV editor panel and vice versa (bidirectional selection sync)
The UV editor panel supports zoom and pan independently of the main viewport
Island outlines are drawn at their exact boundary shape (not only their bounding box rectangle)
The tailor_uv_inspect MCP tool response includes island boundary polygon vertices so a model can inspect island shapes programmatically
UV editor panel is visible and usable without an active simulation run (authoring-time packing preview is sufficient)
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: visual capture of UV editor panel with a 3-panel garment shows 3 distinct non-overlapping island outlines in atlas space
scope.risk_if_missed: Without a UV editor panel, operators and models cannot inspect or diagnose UV layout defects (overlap, low fill ratio, misaligned grain) on a per-island basis. Texture seam issues will not be caught until downstream DCC import, increasing rework.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
